purpose: Classify whether a T435-style A1 quantum E3 boundary survives or collapses when A2 reference/asymmetry resources are audited.
classification_requirements_for_absolute: underlying case clears the T435 A1 relative E3 gate
A2 reference/asymmetry resources are explicitly considered
the A2 policy does not admit a separator that recovers the datum
large resource cost alone is not counted as exact impossibility
an independent exact no-go witness is predeclared
no hidden label, post-hoc resource, or pair-specific selector is used

classifications.description: The T435 main phase pair clears the A1 relative gate, but A2 admits the reference/asymmetry resource that recovers X.
classifications.reason: A2 admits a reference/asymmetry resource that recovers the separator.

classifications.description: The T435 dephased-mixture control is also A1-relative rather than absolute once the same A2 reference policy is admitted.
classifications.reason: A2 admits a reference/asymmetry resource that recovers the separator.

classifications.description: Synthetic calibration: the classifier can report an absolute E3 shape if a predeclared exact no-go still forbids the separator after A2 is considered.
classifications.reason: The A1 obstruction survives the A2 resource-lift audit because a predeclared exact no-go witness still forbids the separator. In this artifact this is synthetic calibration only.

classifications.description: A large or family-dependent resource cost without exact impossibility does not clear the absolute E3 classifier.
classifications.reason: Large or family-dependent resource cost is an E1/E2-style burden, not a single-instance exact E3 no-go.

classifications.description: A candidate that never audits A2 stays A1-relative only.
classifications.reason: The candidate is only classified relative to A1; A2 was not audited.

classifications.description: A resource policy selected after seeing the pair is blocked.
classifications.reason: The resource-lift policy was not frozen before pair selection.

classifications.description: A resource-lift packet that reads a hidden label is blocked.
classifications.reason: The resource-lift packet reads a hidden label or hidden resource.

classifications.description: If A1 already distinguishes the pair, there is no E3 boundary.
classifications.reason: The underlying T435 candidate does not first clear the A1 relative E3 gate: no_boundary_a1_already_distinguishes.

classifications.description: Classical full-code controls remain T433-style declared cases.
classifications.reason: The underlying T435 candidate does not first clear the A1 relative E3 gate: control_not_quantum_e3_regime.

overall_verdict.reading: T436 preserves T435 as an A1 admissible-menu control while blocking the stronger reading that T435 is an absolute E3/no-go result. The T435 phase pair is lifted to E0 once A2 admits the reference resource. Absolute E3 requires a separate exact no-go witness that survives the resource-lift audit.

honest_ceiling: Recorded-tier taxonomy guardrail only. T436 classifies resource-lift status for finite gate cases; it is not a WAY theorem, not a quantum physics claim, not a GU/TaF adapter, not cross-repo evidence, and not claim-ledger movement.
recommended_next: Future quantum E3 attempts should report both A1 status and A2 resource-lift status.
Do not cite T435 as an absolute no-go; it is relative to A1 without reference.
A real absolute E3 artifact must bring an independently typed exact no-go witness.
